title: anisochilus carnosus var. glaber
intro: Anisochilus carnosus var. glaber, also known as the Himalayan sweet root, is a plant species belonging to the Lamiaceae family. It has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in digestion, making it a potential natural remedy for several health conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used in traditional Indian medicine in the form of powder or oil.
